2605.03862.title: Correct Is Not Enough: Training Reasoning Planners with Executor-Grounded Rewards
2605.03862.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has become a common way to improve explicit reasoning in large language models, but final-answer correctness alone does not reveal whether the reasoning trace is faithful, reliable, or useful to the model that consumes it. This outcome-only signal can reinforce traces that are right for the wrong reasons, overstate reasoning gains by rewarding shortcuts, and propagate flawed intermediate states in multi-step systems. To this end, we propose TraceLift, a planner-executor training framework that treats reasoning as a consumable intermediate artifact. During planner training, the planner emits tagged reasoning. A frozen executor turns this reasoning into the final artifact for verifier feedback, while an executor-grounded reward shapes the intermediate trace. This reward multiplies a rubric-based Reasoning Reward Model (RM) score by measured uplift on the same frozen executor, crediting traces that are both high-quality and useful. To make reasoning quality directly learnable, we introduce TRACELIFT-GROUPS, a rubric-annotated reason-only dataset built from math and code seed problems. Each example is a same-problem group containing a high-quality reference trace and multiple plausible flawed traces with localized perturbations that reduce reasoning quality or solution support while preserving task relevance. Extensive experiments on code and math benchmarks show that this executor-grounded reasoning reward improves the two-stage planner-executor system over execution-only training, suggesting that reasoning supervision should evaluate not only whether a trace looks good, but also whether it helps the model that consumes it.
2605.03862.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習與可驗證獎勵已成為改善大型語言模型明確推理的常見方法，但僅僅依賴最終答案的正確性並不能揭示推理過程是否忠實、可靠或對消耗該過程的模型有用。這種僅基於結果的信號可能會強化因錯誤原因而正確的推理過程，通過獎勵捷徑來誇大推理的收益，並在多步驟系統中傳播有缺陷的中間狀態。為此，我們提出了TraceLift，一個將推理視為可消耗中間產物的規劃者-執行者訓練框架。在規劃者訓練期間，規劃者發出標記的推理。一個凍結的執行者將這些推理轉化為最終產物以供驗證者反饋，而一個基於執行者的獎勵則塑造中間推理過程。這個獎勵通過在同一凍結執行者上測量的提升來乘以基於標準的推理獎勵模型（RM）分數，給予既高質量又有用的推理過程以認可。為了使推理質量能夠直接學習，我們引入了TRACELIFT-GROUPS，一個基於數學和代碼種子問題構建的僅包含推理的標準註釋數據集。每個示例是一組相同問題，包含一個高質量的參考推理過程和多個具有局部擾動的合理有缺陷推理過程，這些擾動降低了推理質量或解決方案支持，同時保持了任務的相關性。對代碼和數學基準的廣泛實驗表明，這種基於執行者的推理獎勵改善了兩階段的規劃者-執行者系統，相較於僅基於執行的訓練，這表明推理監督不僅應評估推理過程是否看起來良好，還應評估它是否有助於消耗該過程的模型。
2605.03862.author: Tianyang Han et.al.
2605.03862.authors: Tianyang Han, Hengyu Shi, Junjie Hu, Xu Yang, Zhiling Wang, Junhao Su
